skill.name: hmdb-database
skill.description: Use the Human Metabolome Database for metabolite lookup, structure searches, spectra matching, biomarker review, and bulk dataset planning.
skill.summary: Guide metabolomics research with HMDB search, field, spectra, and dataset references.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography hits, but review found they are Markdown field names, HMDB terminology, and XML examples. The only confirmed risk factor is normal network awareness from links to the public HMDB website and downloads page. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or autonomous command execution was found.

low_findings.title: Public HMDB Network References
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE, low risk. The skill points users to the public HMDB website and downloads page. These are expected database references, not hidden callbacks or exfiltration endpoints. Confidence: 0.90. Confidence reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links to hmdb.ca, and no code sends local data to them.

low_findings.title: External Command Detections Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks around HMDB field names, an R package install example, and a reference filename. These lines are documentation, not Ruby or shell execution. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The reviewed files contain no executable scripts, no command substitution, and no dynamic command construction.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines describe HMDB content, compound descriptions, HTTP-based querying, or related databases. No weak hash, cipher, password storage, or cryptographic implementation appears in the skill. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The files are Markdown references and contain no cryptographic code paths or security-sensitive transformations.

content.value_statement: Metabolomics work often requires reliable metabolite identifiers, spectra, pathways, and biomarker context. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through HMDB searches, data fields, downloads, and interpretation workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use HMDB for metabolomics, clinical chemistry, and biomarker research.
Guides searches by metabolite name, HMDB ID, disease association, structure, spectrum, and specimen type.
Summarizes key HMDB data fields for chemistry, biology, pathways, biomarkers, spectra, and external identifiers.
Describes bulk download formats such as XML, SDF, FASTA, TXT, CSV, and TSV.
Outlines workflows for metabolite identification, biomarker discovery, pathway analysis, and database integration.
Documents limitations of public API access and points users toward approved programmatic options.
limitations: It does not include a live HMDB API client or automated data retrieval code.
It depends on the current availability and licensing terms of the HMDB website.
It cannot validate clinical claims without primary literature and expert review.
Some HMDB fields are sparse, predicted, or unavailable for specific metabolites.

use_cases.title: Identify Unknown Metabolites
use_cases.description: Use HMDB search methods to compare names, masses, structures, NMR data, and MS spectra against reference entries.

use_cases.title: Review Biomarker Evidence
use_cases.description: Find disease associations, normal concentration ranges, specimen context, and cited evidence for candidate biomarkers.

use_cases.title: Plan Local Data Integration
use_cases.description: Select HMDB download formats and field groups for pipelines, databases, and cross-reference mapping.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Metabolite Entry
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner lookup by name or identifier
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the HMDB database skill to help me look up [metabolite name or HMDB ID]. Summarize identifiers, formula, molecular weight, synonyms, and major biological context.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Identification Evidence
prompt_templates.prompt: Use HMDB guidance to evaluate candidate metabolites for an unknown feature with mass [mass], specimen [specimen], and optional spectra notes [notes]. List evidence to check and likely disqualifiers.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Biomarker Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Use HMDB fields to review whether [metabolite] is associated with [disease or condition]. Include concentration context, biospecimen relevance, pathway links, and evidence limitations.

prompt_templates.title: Design an HMDB Data Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the HMDB database skill to design a local data integration plan for [research goal]. Recommend download formats, field groups, identifier mappings, version tracking, and quality checks.

output_examples.input: Look up 1-methylhistidine in HMDB for a metabolomics report.
output_examples.output: A concise metabolite brief with HMDB identifier, common names, formula, molecular weight, specimen locations, pathway context, and evidence notes.

output_examples.input: Help evaluate whether a serum feature could be a disease biomarker.
output_examples.output: A review checklist covering disease links, normal and abnormal concentration ranges, specimen fit, cited references, and confidence limits.

output_examples.input: Plan a pipeline that maps HMDB metabolites to KEGG and PubChem.
output_examples.output: A data integration plan naming suitable HMDB downloads, useful identifier fields, version records, validation checks, and expected missing data.

best_practices: Record the HMDB version and access date for every analysis.
Confirm identifications with multiple evidence types, including mass, spectra, structure, and specimen plausibility.
Use bulk downloads for large studies instead of repeated manual website queries.
anti_patterns: Do not treat predicted spectra or concentrations as equivalent to experimental evidence.
Do not use HMDB biomarker associations as medical advice without expert validation.
Do not scrape HMDB at scale when approved downloads or access channels are available.

faq.question: Does this skill query HMDB automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for using HMDB web tools, downloads, and data fields.

faq.question: Can it help with metabolite identification?
faq.answer: Yes. It outlines how to compare names, identifiers, masses, structures, specimen context, and spectra.

faq.question: Does HMDB provide a public REST API?
faq.answer: The skill states that HMDB does not provide a public REST API and recommends contacting HMDB for programmatic access.

faq.question: What data formats does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers XML, SDF, FASTA, TXT, CSV, TSV, and spectra-related downloads.

faq.question: Can it support clinical decisions?
faq.answer: No. It can organize database evidence, but clinical interpretation requires qualified expert review.

faq.question: Is commercial use covered?
faq.answer: The skill notes that commercial use requires explicit permission from HMDB contacts.
